How Often Should I Read the Qurn?
 
Shaykh Muammad bin Al bin dam al-Ityb   said:

 this shows that, at the very least, reading the [entire] Qurn should be done monthly; every night, you should read one juz until you complete it at the end of the month. This is the longest period of time it should be. Then, if you have desire, in twenty, fifteen, and so on, and do not do it more than every seven days; seven days was the standard of the Salaf: the majority of the Salaf used to read the Qurn in seven parts; they split it into seven portions, and so they would finish it every week.

Tr: Ab Hurayrah al-Birn

"Upon the authority of Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allh (sallallhu 'alaihi wa sallam) said: 

When the month of Ramadan enters, the doors of Paradise are opened, the doors of Hellfire are closed, and the (chief) devils are chained.
[Bukhari, Muslim]

Shaykh Ibn Baz (May Allh have mercy on him) mentioned in explanation of the above hadith:

"This indicates the virtue of this great month and that Allah, Glorified and Exalted, grants His servants success in performing many righteous deeds during it, deeds for which the doors of Paradise are opened and the doors of the heavens are opened, as occurs in another narration: The doors of the heavens are opened.

In this is an encouragement to increase in acts of goodness such as prayer, giving charity, saying Subhnallh, saying L ilha illallh, and other than that to seize this opportunity; the opportunity of this great month and what it contains of Allahs generosity, His kindness, and the vastness of His benevolence.

Likewise, (seizing the opportunity found in) the chaining of the (chief) devils and their inability to reach what they used to reach (of evil) in other months, and that their evil is weakened."

 Explanation of Shaykh ibn Baz on Riyd As-Slihn (2nd explanation, no. 389) - taken from the Shaykh's website."

"Marriage, wealth, health, Hajj, death, food, clothing - everything.

On Laylat al-Qadr, the decrees for the coming year are transferred from al-Lawh al-Mahfooz.

A man once asked al-Hasan while I was listening,

 "Is Laylat al-Qadr in every Ramadan?"

He replied,

 "Yes, by Allah, besides whom there is no god, it is in every Ramadan. On Laylat al-Qadr, every wise matter is decreed. Allah determines every lifespan, deed, and provision until the next Laylat al-Qadr.""

How many have sought Allah's help in desperation, only for Him to grant them relief with His abundant blessings!

"A tremendous evening with our brothers and sisters in London at @_MuSunnah in Alperton (Thursday 9th April 2026). 

Msh-Allh, the Markaz was filled with listeners. With barely a days notice, Ahlus-Sunnah travelled from all across London and beyond to join us. The evening began with a dars by Shaikh @uwaysT followed by Shaikh @AbuIdrees and finally a reminder from myself. 

It is important that we learn and convey this Deen with sincerity and good manners, becoming good examples for those around usthat we are humble and distant from the corrupting influences of social mediathat we take the Messenger Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as our example, and then his Companions (Allah be pleased with them)that we love those those who love and follow them, for they are Ahlul-Hadth was-Sunnahand thereafter, the foremost among them in every age are the scholars (may Allah raise their rank). I can say that over the years, we have benefited greatly from the noble manners of the ulamshaping the way we behave and deal with the peopleand I seek pardon from Allah for my shortcomings.

So, in the West, our communities should focus on seeking knowledge; then act on what we learn, cultivate our children, nurture righteousness in our homes and hold fast to the Sunnah. 

Furthermore, Hijrah should be an important discussion in our households: actively planning and searching for a home in a Muslim country where there are already Salafi communities, however small. 

Know that Hijrah from non-Muslim lands to the lands of Islam is an obligationand a protection for you, your family and children; a safety net against the abandonment of religion today and in generations to come, insh-Allh. 

I also advise that you should not waste your time (and wealth) following the fashions, sinful habits and clothing of the non-Muslims because Islam has its own unique and proud heritage and identitynor should you follow and obsess over the modern-day offerings of entertainment (such as football, boxing and movies) that are designed to divert people away from the realities of life and its purpose: the worship of Allah and following the Messenger (peace and blessings be upon him). Focus, O Muslim, on what benefits you in this life and the Hereafter, brakallhu feekum. 

Do not be impressed by social media influencers who seek only views, likes and followers. Our example is the Prophet of Islamwho taught us to be humble, truthful and sincerethen his Companions and the Righteous Salaf. In these times you see that example reflected in the manners of the scholars and their conduct (may Allah preserve the living and have mercy upon the deceased). 

So, I advise you to get off your mobile devices and drastically limit viewing social media platformsinstead read books, attend duroos, get married, have children, focus on your families and build true Islamic homes. 

May Allah aid the Muslims and guide them to the Sunnah and the path of Ahlul-Hadthand that is the Madhhab of the Salaf. 

In thisby Allahthere is honour, just as Umar (Allah be pleased with him) said: We were a lowly people, and Allah honoured us with Islam. So if we seek honour in other than Islam, then Allah will make us lowly.

Abu Khadeejah."

(84) 
The Claim That Shaykh Al-Albani Was Overly Lenient and Shaykh Muqbil Overly Strict in Hadith Grading

Question: 
It is said about Shaykh Nasir Ad-Din Al-Albani that he was lenient in his authentication and weakening of hadiths, and about Shaykh Muqbil that he was overly severe. How true are these statements?

Sheikh Yahya Al-Hajoori
As for the claim that Shaykh Al-Albani was lenient in authentication and weakeningthis is incorrect. How could that be? He never weakened a hadith without providing clear evidence for its weakness. He was extremely meticulous in weakening hadiths. His book Silsilah Adh-Daifah (The Chain of Weak Hadiths) stands as a testament to this, showing his great precision. Regarding authentication, he would sometimes exercise his own reasoning (ijtihad) on certain hadiths, citing various chains and applying his own methodological principles. He might say, "This hadith, through these chains, is hasan in my view." This is similar to what Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar does in Bulugh Al-Maram, where he says, "This hadith is hasan," even though some other hadith masters or researchers may disagree. The same can be said for Az-Zaylai and other scholarsthis is a normal matter of scholarly difference.

It is true that some hadiths in Silsilah As-Sahihah (The Chain of Authentic Hadiths) which he deemed authentic are not accepted by some other scholars. However, in reality, his ijtihad was very strong, and he provided the Muslim Ummah with an immense treasure that neither a scholar nor a student can do without. He remains a mujtahid, like other mujtahids in the field of hadith. A student of knowledge should still examine the hadiths, even those found in Silsilah As-Sahihah or Irwa Al-Ghalil, and form their own conclusion based on what they see, even if it aligns with Shaykh Al-Albanis ruling. If a student feels they have not reached the level to judge a hadith as hasan or sahih as the Shaykh did, they can simply hold their own informed opinion. And all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

As for the claim that Shaykh Muqbil was severe in authentication and weakeningthere is no doubt that he was extremely meticulous. The "severity" here simply refers to his rigorous precision. Shaykh Muqbil would scrutinise many hadiths in light of hidden defects (ilal). I once asked him directly: "O Shaykh, the hadiths you included in As-Sahih Al-Musnad, did you examine them for hidden defects?" He replied, "I mostly examine them for hidden defects." He continued this practice throughout his life, constantly reviewing As-Sahih Al-Musnad against books of ilal. If Al-Hafidh Ad-Daraqutni, Abu Hatim, Abu Zurah, Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar, or any of the hadith masters stated that a hadith had a defect, Shaykh Muqbil would either remove it from his collection or keep it with a note indicating its defect.

In my observation, the criticisms regarding authentication directed at our Shaykh (Muqbil), may Allah have mercy on him, are far fewer than those directed at Shaykh Al-Albani. This is worth noting, considering the vast number of hadiths that Shaykh Al-Albani ruled upon compared to the number that our Shaykh ruled upon.

" Ancient DNA Sample  Simple Summary
From #Akbari 2026

Published before Disparate demographic impacts of the Roman Colonization and the Migration Period in the Iberian Peninsula, deposited on bioRxiv under the DOI 10.1101/2024.09.23.614606.

This individual was a male buried around 623 AD in a cave burial site in northeastern #Spain (Girona, #Catalonia) during the Visigothic period.

Genetic Background

 Fathers lineage (Y-DNA): J1-L858
This lineage comes from the ancient Near East, especially regions like the Levant and Arabia, and is commonly linked to Semitic populations.

 Mothers lineage (mtDNA): H1
This is a typical Western European lineage, especially common in Iberia, showing local ancestry on the maternal side.

 Overall Ancestry

His genome shows a strong connection to eastern Mediterranean populations, especially groups historically related to:

Jewish communities of North Africa

Southern Europe (Italy, Greece)

His deeper ancestry is made up of:

Early farmers from Anatolia (Turkey region)

Significant Levantine/Natufian ancestry (ancient Near Eastern hunter-gatherers)

Smaller inputs from Iranian farmers, Caucasus groups, and European hunter-gatherers

What This Means

This man was not genetically Visigothic in the northern European sense.

Instead, he represents a mixed Mediterranean profile:

A Near Eastern paternal line

A local Iberian maternal line

A genome similar to eastern Mediterranean / North African Jewish populations

 Likely Historical Scenario

He was probably descended from people who moved west during the Roman Empire, such as:

Soldiers

Traders

Migrants from the eastern Mediterranean

These groups settled in Iberia and mixed with local populations. By the Visigothic period, their descendants were fully integrated culturally, even if their genetic origins were diverse.

 Key Insight

The term Visigothic here reflects culture and political identity, not a uniform genetic background.
This individual is a clear example of how migration and mixing in the Roman world shaped the population of early medieval Spain.

Doubt: Shaykh Muqbil ibn Haadi, was a takfiri or made Takfir on Saudi

Responded by his student, Shaykh AbdulHameed az-Zukuri  :

Yahya Silmi As-Saylani (the Sri Lankan) says:
Sheikh Muqbils initial opinion, which caused a great uproar, was: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. He was firmly set on this statement, announcing it in Friday sermons. Then, when he went to Saudi Arabia before his death, he sat with its scholars, and they convinced him that it is a Salafi country, so he retracted and acknowledged that it is a pure Muslim country. [From the tape: Explanation of My Advice to Ahlus-Sunnah].

Sheikh Abdulhamid Az-Zukari responds:
So on Friday, the twenty-fifth of Rajab, in the year 1433 AH, we respond to this false statement, falsely and aggressively attributed to our sheikh, our father, and our teacher, Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, may Allah Almighty have mercy on himthe one whom Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed and favoured by making him a reviver of this religion alongside other revivers, such as Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, and Sheikh Al-Uthaymin, may Allah have mercy on him. We have spoken about his way, his methodology, his biography, and some of what he adhered to in our book Al-Bayan Al-Hasan fi Tarjamat Al-Imam Al-Wadii wa Ma Ahyahu min As-Sunan. And we have responded to those who claim that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was a takfiri at any time or period in the book Awn Al-Bari li Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari. In that book, we explained the gist: that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was never a Haruri or a Khariji; rather, he was a Sunni Salafi ever since Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed him with the knowledge of the creed of the Salaf, Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamaah. Thus, he became a caller to it and a reviver of it. 

Among the evidence that shows the falsehood of Yahya Silmis statementthat Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, said about the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia that it is a kafir countryare the following matters:

First: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, refuted Osama ibn Laden, refuted Abu Muhammad Al-Maqdisi, refuted Al-Masari, and spoke against Salman, Safar, and those similar to them who hold takfiri statements and deviant methodologies.

Second: We are certain and convinced that our sheikh, may Allah have mercy on him, never said this statement at any time because it contradicts reality, and Sheikh Muqbil was among the people most aware of the condition of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabiamay Allah grant it success and protect it with Tawhid.

Third: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah have mercy on him, did not apply this description to countries that are less upright and less devoted to spreading good, let alone countries containing graves and shrines worshipped besides Allah, despite his belief that those who engage in grave-worship, those who call upon another god alongside Allah, those who slaughter for other than Allah, and those who make vows for other than Allah are disbelievers.

Fourth: Such an absolute statement is questionable. Even if we were to suppose that in that country there are acts of disbelief, or in Yemen there are acts of disbelief, or in Sudan there are acts of disbelief, or in Iraq there are acts of disbelief, this kind of absolute statementcalling any of the Muslim countries a kafir country outrightis questionable. This claim is contradicted by Sheikh Muqbils tapes, contradicted by the actual stance of Sheikh Muqbil, and contradicted by the students of Sheikh Muqbil.

Fifth: If Sheikh Muqbil had ever uttered this statement or something similar, the scholars would have condemned him for it.

Either Yahya Silmi received this statement from idle, malicious opponents of the call of Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, which has filled the plains and mountains, or he heard something similar to what Abdullah Al-Bukhari statedthat Sheikh Muqbil repented from the methodology of khuruj in the sense of his words two months before his deathand due to his poor command of Arabic and ignorance, he assumed they were accusing Sheikh Muqbil of saying that Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. It is sufficient to show a persons misguidance that he be a liar in what he transmits or inarticulate in his speech, for the inarticulate cannot express what is in his heart nor clarify what he intends, thus falling into something worse than what he wishes to deny. His situation is like what some of the Salaf said about Umar ibn Ubayd: Your shortcoming came from your poor Arabic, when he said, It is incumbent upon Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, to fulfill the threat. So I advise Yahya Silmi to fear Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, and I advise him to be humble and to seek knowledge.

The second matter: His statement, He sat with its scholars and they convinced himwhat did they convince him of? The fact is that it was conveyed to him, may Allah have mercy on him, that there was some injustice in Saudi Arabia and such things, so he would condemn that specific injustice that was reported to him; otherwise, he remained upon the Salafi creed. Evidence for this is that Sheikh Ibn Baz, Sheikh Ibn Uthaymin, Sheikh Rabee, Sheikh An-Najmi, and Sheikh Al-Albaninone of them, not in a single letter, accused Sheikh Muqbil of holding the opinion of the Khawarij or the takfiris, despite their knowledge of what he said or uttered, whether in what he wrote in Gharat Al-Ashirat or what he wrote in Al-Makhraj min Al-Fitnah or what he stated in other books. Then he retracted, may Allah have mercy on him, before his death; he retracted from all statements he had made about Saudi Arabia in that tape entitled My Observations in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia when he saw and heard the good in the country, as he explained the reason for his retraction.

The point of this introduction is: I declare this transmission and this statement from Yahya Silmi to be false. Sheikh Muqbil did not declare the Saudi government to be disbelievers nor did he say it is a kafir country. Scholars condemned Sayyid Qutb when he called it the Jahiliyyah of the twentieth century, and then Yahya Silmi comes and attributes such a statement to an imam of the Salafi call and a reviver of the Prophetic Sunnah, claiming that he used to say Saudi Arabia is a disbelieving country. So check your sources, O you who fabricates! 
{ } 
Cursed be the liars! 
Review the Arabic language and master it. If something is unclear to you, be humble and ask until you understand. But to become a fabricator who falsely attributes statementsand to whom?! To an imam known for the soundness of his creed and methodology! We did not accept from Abdullah Al-Bukhari nor from anyone else who claims that Sheikh Muqbil repented from khuruj. We say that Sheikh Muqbil never held the view of khuruj, nor do we accept that, neither at the beginning of his pursuit of knowledge nor at the end. Sheikh Muqbil was a Sunni Salafi from the time Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, enlightened him with the Book and the Sunnah. He denied this about himself, truthfully in what he said and conveyed, that he was never with Juhayman at any time, nor was he a supporter of him; rather, he condemned him, and so on. I refer to what I wrote in my book Awn Al-Bari fi Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari, and added to it is the refutation of the fabrications of Yahya Silmi Al-Saylani.

""A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Sh. Sulayman ar-Ruhayli
"Alhamdulillah can understand now (not entirely though)

Advice from Shaykh Abu Dujanah   (senior student of Imam Muqbil):

The Reprehensible Acts of Eid!
There are many violations during Eid that many people fall into. We will mention some of them so that we can be wary of them and avoid falling into them. These include:
1. Adorning oneself by shaving the beard: Know, my brother, that shaving the beard is forbidden (Haram) and a sin. The evidence for this is the hadith of Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches and let the beards grow.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5393).
And from Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches, and let the beards grow long; differ from the Magians.)  Narrated by Muslim (260).
Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this wording in the mentioned hadiths necessitates the obligation of growing and lengthening the beards, and the prohibition of shaving and cutting them, because the default principle regarding commands is obligation, and the default principle regarding prohibitions is prohibition, unless there is evidence indicating otherwise, and this is what is relied upon by the people of knowledge.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/372).
> 
2. Dyeing the hair black: Know, my brother, that it is not permissible to dye the hair black, and a severe threat has been reported for those who do so. From Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (There will be a people at the end of time who will dye their hair black like the crops of pigeons; they will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.)  Narrated by Ahmad (2470), and Abu Dawud (4212).
> (So this hadith is explicit regarding the prohibition of dyeing with black.)  Tuhfat Al-Ahwadhi (5/359).
> 
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this hadith necessitates the prohibition of dyeing gray hair black, and that it is from the major sins.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (11/123).
> 
3. Isbal (Lengthening the garment below the ankles): Isbal of the lower garment is one of the major sins because a severe threat is attached to it, and the one who practices Isbal has fallen into two prohibitions:
 * The First: He has exposed himself to punishment. From Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, who said: (Whatever of the lower garment is below the ankles is in the Fire.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5787).
 * The Second: He has exposed his garment to dirt and filth. A young man wearing a long garment entered upon Umar bin Al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, and he said to him: (O son of my brother, raise your garment, for it is cleaner for your garment, and more pious toward your Lord.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari in the story of the assassination of Umar (3700).
The Sunnah is for the lower garment and clothing of the Muslim to be at mid-calf. From Al-Alaa bin Abdul Rahman, from his father, that he said: I asked Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri about the lower garment, and he said: I will inform you with knowledge; I heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, say: (The lower garment of the believer is to the middle of his calves. There is no sin on him for what is between that and the ankles. Whatever is lower than that is in the Fire, whatever is lower than that is in the Fire. Allah will not look on the Day of Resurrection at the one who drags his lower garment out of arrogance.)  Narrated by Malik in Al-Muwatta (2/914).
So the Muslim must beware of Isbal.

4. Shaking hands with non-Mahram (unrelated) women: This is an affliction that has become widespread, and no one has escaped it except those whom Allah has shown mercy. It is prohibited due to what was narrated by Abdullah bin Al-Shikhkhir who said: I heard Ma'qil bin Yasar say: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (For one of you to be stabbed in the head with an iron needle is better for him than to touch a woman who is not permissible for him.)  Narrated by Al-Tabarani in Al-Mu'jam Al-Kabeer (486).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And in the hadith, there is a severe threat for the one who touches a woman who is not permissible for him; thus, it contains evidence for the prohibition of shaking hands with women, because that is undoubtedly included in touching.)  As-Silsilah As-Saheehah (1/448).
> 
> (So this hadith indicates explicitly the prohibition of touching a non-Mahram woman, and from this is: shaking hands; as the Lawgiver has attached a severe threat to touching a woman who is not permissible for him, which is: being stabbed with an iron needle. Attaching a severe threat to an action indicates its prohibition.)  Al-Musarahah fi Ahkam Al-Musafahah (p.62).
> 
5. Free mixing of men and women: This often occurs in parks and recreational areas, and there is much evidence regarding the prohibition of the free mixing of men and women, so the Muslim must be wary of that. From Abu Usaid Malik bin Rabi'ah, may Allah be pleased with him, that he heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saywhile he was coming out of the mosque, and men and women had mixed in the street: (Draw back, for you must not walk in the middle of the street; keep to the sides of the street. So women used to cling to the wall so much that their garments would catch on the wall due to their clinging to it.)  Narrated by Abu Dawud (5272).
Sheikh Muhammad bin Ibrahim, the former Grand Mufti of the Saudi Lands, said:
> (And the point of evidence is: that if the Messenger of Allahpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyprevented mixing in the street because it leads to temptation, then how can it be said that mixing is permissible elsewhere?!)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (10/42).
> 
Ali bin Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, said: (Do you not feel ashamed or have jealousy? For it has reached me that your women go out into the markets, crowding the 'Ulooj (1).)  Narrated by Ahmad (1118).
(1) Al-'Ulooj: Plural of 'Ilj, which refers to a disbelieving man of non-Arab descent.
Sheikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And it was from the Sunnah of the Prophetpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyand the Sunnah of his successors to separate men and women... because the mixing of one group with the other is a cause for temptation; for when men mix with women, it is like mixing fire and wood.)  Al-Istiqamah (1/360).
> 
6. Women leaving their homes perfumed and scented: This action is not permissible; rather, it has come in the hadith that whoever does this is a fornicator. From Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari who said: The Messenger of Allah - peace and blessings be upon him - said: (Any woman who puts on perfume and passes by a people so they can smell her fragrance, is a fornicator.)  Narrated by An-Nasa'i (9361), and Ibn Khuzaymah (1681), and Al-Albani said: Its chain of narration is Hasan (good).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (Al-Haytami mentioned in 'Az-Zawajir' 2/37 that a woman leaving her home perfumed and adorned is among the major sins, even if her husband permitted her.)  Jilbab Al-Mar'ah Al-Muslimah (p.139).
>

7. Buying and selling firecrackers: Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (What I see is that buying and selling them is Haram (prohibited), and that is for two reasons. The first reason: it is a waste of money, and wasting money is prohibited due to the Prophet's, peace and blessings be upon him, forbidding of that. The second: it causes harm to people with its disturbing noises, and it may cause fires if it falls on something flammable while it is alive [lit] and not extinguished.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/3).
> 
8. Al-Qaza': Which is shaving part of the head and leaving part of it: From Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both: (That the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, forbade Al-Qaza'.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5921), and Muslim (2120).
And from him, may Allah be pleased with him, he said: (The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saw a boy who had part of his hair shaved and part of it left, so he forbade them from that and said: 'Shave it all, or leave it all.')  Narrated by Abu Dawud (4195).
It is said in Al-Qamoos:
> (Al-Qaza' is to shave the head of a boy and leave separate spots un-shaved, resembling the Qaza' (scattered patches) of clouds, and similarly in An-Nihayah.)  Ghidha' Al-Albab (1/429).
> 
Sheikh Abdul Mohsen Al-Abbad, may Allah preserve him, said in his explanation of Sunan Abi Dawud:
> (And included in Al-Qaza' is what some youth do in barbershops by shaving the hair at the nape of the neck seeking adornment; because shaving part of the head and leaving part of it leads to the same result, whether it is intended for adornment or not intended for adornment, except if the shaving is for medical treatment or for Hijamah (cupping), then there is no harm in this.)
> 
9. Girls wearing short clothing:
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (I see that a person should not dress his daughter in this short clothing while she is young, because if she gets used to it, she will remain upon it and its matter will become trivial to her. As for if she is accustomed to modesty from her youth, she will remain in that state in her adulthood. And what I advise our Muslim sisters is to abandon the clothing of the people abroad from the enemies of the religion, and to accustom their daughters to covering clothing, and to modesty, for modesty is from faith.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (12/274).
> 
10. Not listening to the Eid sermon: Some people, after praying the Eid prayer, leave and do not listen to the sermon, and this is a mistake. Imam Malik was asked about a man who prays with the Imam on the day of Al-Fitr, is it permissible for him to leave before hearing the sermon? He said: (He should not leave until the Imam leaves.)  Ahkam Al-'Idayn by Al-Faryabi (144).
 There are many violations that occur during Eid; we have mentioned some of them. I ask Allah to benefit me and you through them, and Allah knows best.

A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same.

In a Al-Bukhr, from Ab Ubayd, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, who said: I witnessed the two Eids with Uthmn ibn Affn on a Friday. He performed the prayer before delivering the sermon, then said: O people, today two Eids have coincided for you. Whoever among the residents of the outskirts (Al-awl) wishes to wait for the Jumu'ah, let him do so; whoever wishes to return home, I give him permission.

Based on these authentic prophetic traditions and reliable reports from the pious predecessors, the majority of scholars have concluded the following:

First: Whoever attends the Eid prayer is granted a concession from attending the Jumu'ah prayer. He may instead pray the Dhuhr prayer at its time. However, if he chooses to attend the Jumuah prayer with the congregation, that is more virtuous and better.

Second: Whoever did not attend the Eid prayer is not included in the concession. Therefore, the obligation of Jumuah remains upon him, and he must go to the masjid for the Jumuah prayer. If the minimum number required to establish Jumu'ah is not found, he performs Dhuhr instead.

Third: The Imm of the masjid is obliged to hold the Jumuah prayer that day, so that those who wish to attend it or those who did not attend the Eid prayer may do so. This is the explicit statement of the Prophet : Indeed, we are going to establish Jumuah prayer.

Fourth: Whoever attended the Eid prayer and avails himself of the concession not to attend Jumuah must pray Dhuhr. The Dhuhr prayer does not fall away under any circumstance, because what is lifted is the obligation of Jumuahnot of Dhuhr. Those upon whom Jumuah is not obligatorysuch as the sick, the traveller, women, and childrenare unanimously required by the scholars to perform Dhuhr.

Fifth: The opinion that whoever attends the Eid prayer is exempt from both the Jumuah and the Dhuhr prayers for that day is a weak opinion, though attributed to some Companions such as Ibn Az-Zubayr and Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them). This conclusion may be explained in several ways:

First, perhaps those who held this view were unaware of the adths that explicitly grant a concession from attending Jumuah, but affirm the obligation of Dhuhr for those who do not attend Jumuah.

Second, Ibn Az-Zubayr may have combined the Eid and Jumuah prayers at the time of the early Jumuah (before the sun reached its zenith). This is supported by a narration from Jbir ibn Abdullh (may Allh be pleased with him): We used to perform the prayer with the Messenger of Allh , then return and let our camels rest. This was reported by Muslim. Also, Sahl (may Allh be pleased with him) said: We would neither nap nor eat lunch until after Jumuah. This is the position of Imm Amad and Isq ibn Rhawayh (may Allh have mercy on them).

Additional evidence indicating that Ibn Az-Zubayr did this includes narrations such as: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the morning. We then went for Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we told him, he said: He followed the Sunnah. In another narration: A Friday and Eid coincided under Ibn Az-Zubayr, who said: Two Eids have come together in one day. So he combined them into one, prayed two rakahs early in the morning, and did not add anything until he prayed Ar.

This is a clear and explicit text that he included the Jumuah within the Eid prayer. And whoever performs the Jumuah does not then perform Dhuhr. Therefore, when this was reported to Ibn Abbs, he said: He acted upon the Sunnah.

This, dear brothers and sisters, is a concise yet beneficial explanation regarding the concurrence of Jumuah and Eid. I ask Allh, the Exalted, to have guided me to speak in accordance with the Sunnah and to have brought clarity on this issue to the general Muslim public...
